{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Trainee - Front Desk (Female)",
  "description": "Resurgir Consulting - Our client is a renewable energy solutions provider offering both on-grid and off-grid power solutions. It serves individuals, SMEs, government institutions, and other organizations seeking cost-effective, reliable, and sustainable electricity solutions.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Trainee - Front Desk (Female)

Job Location: Kaura District, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full Time ( Onsite)
Start Date: 1st July
Work Schedule: Monday - Friday, 9:00 AM &ndash; 5:00 PM

Job Summary


	The Executive Trainee &ndash; Front Desk will serve as the first point of contact for clients and visitors, ensuring a professional customer experience while providing administrative and customer support functions.
	The successful candidate will support daily office operations, maintain accurate records, and contribute to excellent customer service delivery.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to visitors professionally.
	Manage incoming calls, emails, and inquiries.
	Schedule appointments and coordinate meetings.
	Maintain accurate customer and office records.
	Handle customer complaints and follow-up activities.
	Support documentation, filing, and record management.
	Utilize CRM and digital tools effectively.
	Prepare reports and administrative correspondence.
	Maintain confidentiality of company information.
	Provide general administrative support as required.


Requirements


	HND / B.Sc. Degree in a relevant discipline.
	1 - 2 years of relevant experience.
	Basic solar energy literacy.
	Knowledge of CRM and digital productivity tools.
	Strong customer service skills.
	Customer empathy and conflict-resolution ability.
	Good numeracy skills.
	Strong documentation and record-keeping skills.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Professional appearance and attitude.New Job Order
	Age Range: 24&ndash;28 Years.


Salary Range
N100,000 &ndash; N150,000 / Month.

Method of Appliction&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: applyresurgir@gmail.com using &ldquo;EXECUTIVE TRAINEE &ndash; FRONT DESK (FEMALE) as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-13",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-21",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Resurgir Consulting"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Abuja",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "200392"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 75000,
      "maxValue": 150000,

    }

}



Executive Trainee - Front Desk (Female) Job at Resurgir Consulting




        
    

        
        Resurgir Consulting - Our client is a renewable energy solutions provider offering both on-grid and off-grid power solutions. It serves individuals, SMEs, government institutions, and other organizations seeking cost-effective, reliable, and sustainable electricity solutions.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Trainee - Front Desk (Female)

Job Location: Kaura District, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full Time ( Onsite)
Start Date: 1st July
Work Schedule: Monday - Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Executive Trainee – Front Desk will serve as the first point of contact for clients and visitors, ensuring a professional customer experience while providing administrative and customer support functions.
	The successful candidate will support daily office operations, maintain accurate records, and contribute to excellent customer service delivery.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to visitors professionally.
	Manage incoming calls, emails, and inquiries.
	Schedule appointments and coordinate meetings.
	Maintain accurate customer and office records.
	Handle customer complaints and follow-up activities.
	Support documentation, filing, and record management.
	Utilize CRM and digital tools effectively.
	Prepare reports and administrative correspondence.
	Maintain confidentiality of company information.
	Provide general administrative support as required.


Requirements


	HND / B.Sc. Degree in a relevant discipline.
	1 - 2 years of relevant experience.
	Basic solar energy literacy.
	Knowledge of CRM and digital productivity tools.
	Strong customer service skills.
	Customer empathy and conflict-resolution ability.
	Good numeracy skills.
	Strong documentation and record-keeping skills.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Professional appearance and attitude.New Job Order
	Age Range: 24–28 Years.


Salary Range
N100,000 – N150,000 / Month.

Method of Appliction 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “EXECUTIVE TRAINEE – FRONT DESK (FEMALE) as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Health and Safety Officer (Transport)",
  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health and Safety Officer (Transport)

Job Location: Obajana DCT, Kogi

Job Overview


	The Health and Safety Officer (Transport) is responsible for ensuring that the transportation operations within Dangote Cement Transport comply with all health and safety regulations.
	The officer&#39;s role includes developing, implementing, and enforcing safety programs specifically tailored for transportation activities.


Responsibilities


	&nbsp;Develop and implement health and safety policies and procedures for transportation activities.
	Conduct regular inspections and audits to ensure compliance with local and federal safety regulations.
	Provide training and guidance to transportation staff on safety practices, emergency procedures, and equipment operation.
	Assess risks and implement mitigation strategies for transportation operations.
	Investigate accidents and incidents to determine root causes and recommend corrective actions.
	Maintain safety records and prepare reports on transportation safety performance.
	Coordinate with local authorities and regulatory bodies regarding transportation safety standards.
	Act as a liaison for safety concerns related to the transport fleet and drivers.
	Monitor the effectiveness of existing safety programs and suggest improvements.
	Participate in safety meetings and present relevant safety data and insights to management.


Requirements


	B.Sc Degree in Safety Management, Environmental Science, or a relevant field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a safety role within the transportation or logistics sector.
	Certification in Occupational Health and Safety (NEBOSH, IOSH, or equivalent).
	Graduate Environmental Science, Industrial Hygiene, or a related discipline, preferably in sciences or engineering.
	Professional Certificate or Diploma in fire, safety, health or environmental management from a recognized institution is an added advantage, e.g NEBOSH, IGC, NVQ Level 6
	Awareness of Fleet safety management
	In-depth knowledge of health and safety regulations related to transportation.


Skills and Behaviors:


	Knowledge in training mentoring and development practices
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills to identify and address potential hazards.
	Ability to speak Hausa is an added advantage
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to develop effective training programs.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficient in MS Office Suite and safety management software.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


&nbsp;Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Paid Time Off
	Training &amp; Development.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-13",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-14",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

Health and Safety Officer (Transport) Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health and Safety Officer (Transport)

Job Location: Obajana DCT, Kogi

Job Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Health and Safety Officer (Transport) is responsible for ensuring that the transportation operations within Dangote Cement Transport comply with all health and safety regulations.
	The officer's role includes developing, implementing, and enforcing safety programs specifically tailored for transportation activities.


Responsibilities


	 Develop and implement health and safety policies and procedures for transportation activities.
	Conduct regular inspections and audits to ensure compliance with local and federal safety regulations.
	Provide training and guidance to transportation staff on safety practices, emergency procedures, and equipment operation.
	Assess risks and implement mitigation strategies for transportation operations.
	Investigate accidents and incidents to determine root causes and recommend corrective actions.
	Maintain safety records and prepare reports on transportation safety performance.
	Coordinate with local authorities and regulatory bodies regarding transportation safety standards.
	Act as a liaison for safety concerns related to the transport fleet and drivers.
	Monitor the effectiveness of existing safety programs and suggest improvements.
	Participate in safety meetings and present relevant safety data and insights to management.


Requirements


	B.Sc Degree in Safety Management, Environmental Science, or a relevant field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a safety role within the transportation or logistics sector.
	Certification in Occupational Health and Safety (NEBOSH, IOSH, or equivalent).
	Graduate Environmental Science, Industrial Hygiene, or a related discipline, preferably in sciences or engineering.
	Professional Certificate or Diploma in fire, safety, health or environmental management from a recognized institution is an added advantage, e.g NEBOSH, IGC, NVQ Level 6
	Awareness of Fleet safety management
	In-depth knowledge of health and safety regulations related to transportation.


Skills and Behaviors:


	Knowledge in training mentoring and development practices
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills to identify and address potential hazards.
	Ability to speak Hausa is an added advantage
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to develop effective training programs.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficient in MS Office Suite and safety management software.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


 Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Paid Time Off
	Training & Development.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Export Sales Manager",
  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Export Sales Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	The Export Sales Manager oversees and expands the company&rsquo;s international sales operations for PP woven sacks, BOPP bags, FIBC bags, and related woven packaging products.
	The role focuses on growing export business across West Africa, managing export processes, and ensuring compliance with Nigerian and international trade regulations.
	The individual will build strong relationships with overseas buyers, coordinate with internal teams, and achieve export revenue targets.


Duties &amp; Responsibilities
International Market Expansion:


	Identify and develop new export markets, especially within ECOWAS.
	Conduct market research to understand customer needs, competitor activities, pricing trends, and regulatory requirements.
	Generate high-quality leads through trade fairs, export forums, digital platforms, and industry networks.


Export Sales &amp; Business Growth:


	Develop and execute export sales strategies to meet monthly and annual revenue targets.
	Prepare RFQs, quotations, pricing proposals, and negotiate commercial terms with foreign buyers.
	Manage key accounts, distributors, trading firms, and end-users in sectors like agriculture, cement, fertilizers, and manufacturing.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Maintain regular communication with overseas customers regarding orders, production timelines, quality standards, and shipment status.
	Handle customer enquiries, technical clarifications, product customizations, and order follow-ups.
	Resolve complaints promptly and coordinate corrective actions with production and quality control teams.


Export Operations &amp; Documentation:


	Oversee all export documentation including:
	
		Commercial invoices
		Packing lists
		Certificate of Origin (NACCIMA/NEPC)
		Export Proceeds Form (NXP)
		Bill of Lading / Air Waybill
		Letters of Credit &amp; bank negotiation
	
	
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC), Customs, and international trade regulations.
	Coordinate shipping logistics, freight forwarders, vessel booking, and export clearance procedures.


Coordination With Internal Teams:


	Work with production planning for efficient scheduling and timely export delivery.
	Liaise with the QA/QC team to ensure sacks meet international standards and buyer specifications.
	Collaborate with finance to manage LC documentation, export proceeds (E-FORM), and payment follow-ups.


Reporting &amp; Analysis:


	Maintain accurate export sales reports, forecasts, and customer data.
	Present monthly sales performance, market insights, and action plans to senior management.
	Track export profitability, contribution margins, and logistics costs.


Job Requirements
Qualifications and Work Experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in International Business, Marketing, Business Administration, or relevant field.
	Minimum of 8 years of export sales experience, preferably in PP woven sacks, FIBC, industrial packaging, plastics manufacturing, or FMCG supply chain.
	MBA or certification in export management is a plus.
	Strong knowledge of export procedures, Nigerian customs regulations, Incoterms, and international logistics.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and presentation skills.
	Familiarity with West African markets and packaging industry trends.
	Proficiency in MS Office and ERP systems.
	Willingness to travel internationally.


Skill &amp; Competences:


	Export compliance &amp; documentation
	International sales &amp; negotiation
	Market entry strategy
	Customer Relationship Management
	Pricing and commercial acumen
	Cross-cultural communication
	Data analysis &amp; reporting
	Problem Solving.
",

Export Sales Manager Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Export Sales Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Export Sales Manager oversees and expands the company’s international sales operations for PP woven sacks, BOPP bags, FIBC bags, and related woven packaging products.
	The role focuses on growing export business across West Africa, managing export processes, and ensuring compliance with Nigerian and international trade regulations.
	The individual will build strong relationships with overseas buyers, coordinate with internal teams, and achieve export revenue targets.


Duties & Responsibilities
International Market Expansion:


	Identify and develop new export markets, especially within ECOWAS.
	Conduct market research to understand customer needs, competitor activities, pricing trends, and regulatory requirements.
	Generate high-quality leads through trade fairs, export forums, digital platforms, and industry networks.


Export Sales & Business Growth:


	Develop and execute export sales strategies to meet monthly and annual revenue targets.
	Prepare RFQs, quotations, pricing proposals, and negotiate commercial terms with foreign buyers.
	Manage key accounts, distributors, trading firms, and end-users in sectors like agriculture, cement, fertilizers, and manufacturing.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Maintain regular communication with overseas customers regarding orders, production timelines, quality standards, and shipment status.
	Handle customer enquiries, technical clarifications, product customizations, and order follow-ups.
	Resolve complaints promptly and coordinate corrective actions with production and quality control teams.


Export Operations & Documentation:


	Oversee all export documentation including:
	
		Commercial invoices
		Packing lists
		Certificate of Origin (NACCIMA/NEPC)
		Export Proceeds Form (NXP)
		Bill of Lading / Air Waybill
		Letters of Credit & bank negotiation
	
	
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC), Customs, and international trade regulations.
	Coordinate shipping logistics, freight forwarders, vessel booking, and export clearance procedures.


Coordination With Internal Teams:


	Work with production planning for efficient scheduling and timely export delivery.
	Liaise with the QA/QC team to ensure sacks meet international standards and buyer specifications.
	Collaborate with finance to manage LC documentation, export proceeds (E-FORM), and payment follow-ups.


Reporting & Analysis:


	Maintain accurate export sales reports, forecasts, and customer data.
	Present monthly sales performance, market insights, and action plans to senior management.
	Track export profitability, contribution margins, and logistics costs.


Job Requirements
Qualifications and Work Experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree in International Business, Marketing, Business Administration, or relevant field.
	Minimum of 8 years of export sales experience, preferably in PP woven sacks, FIBC, industrial packaging, plastics manufacturing, or FMCG supply chain.
	MBA or certification in export management is a plus.
	Strong knowledge of export procedures, Nigerian customs regulations, Incoterms, and international logistics.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and presentation skills.
	Familiarity with West African markets and packaging industry trends.
	Proficiency in MS Office and ERP systems.
	Willingness to travel internationally.


Skill & Competences:


	Export compliance & documentation
	International sales & negotiation
	Market entry strategy
	Customer Relationship Management
	Pricing and commercial acumen
	Cross-cultural communication
	Data analysis & reporting
	Problem Solving.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Backend Engineer (Java)",
  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Backend Engineer (Java)

Job Location: Nigeria (Remote)

About the Role


	Engineering at Moniepoint is an inspired, customer-focused community, dedicated to crafting solutions that redefine our industry. Our infrastructure runs on some of the cool tools that excite infrastructure engineers - kubernetes, docker etc.&nbsp;
	We also make business decisions based on the large stream of data we receive daily, so we work daily with big data, perform data analytics and build models to make sense of the noise and give our customers the best experience.&nbsp;


What You&#39;ll Be Doing


	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (product, design, QA) to define, design, and ship high-impact features.
	Design scalable architecture and implement performant backend services using Java and Spring Boot.
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code with a strong focus on reliability and testability.
	Integrate third-party services and APIs while ensuring robust error handling and graceful degradation.
	Continuously improve system performance and developer experience through automation and refactoring.
	Debug complex issues across the stack and proactively identify opportunities for technical improvement.
	Participate in code reviews, architecture discussions, and share best practices with the team.
	Document technical decisions, systems, and processes for future team reference and transparency.


Requirements
What We&#39;re Looking For:


	BS/MS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
	At least 5+ years of professional experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on backend systems.
	Expertise in Java and Spring Boot; solid understanding of modern backend architecture and best practices.
	Experience building and scaling web services, APIs, and distributed systems.
	Familiarity with Agile development methodologies and CI/CD pipelines.
	Proficient with relational databases and ORM frameworks (e.g., Hibernate).
	Exposure to frontend frameworks like AngularJS or React is a plus.
	Strong debugging skills and a methodical approach to problem-solving.
	Excellent communication skills and a team-first attitude.


Our Tech Stack
As part of our engineering team, you&rsquo;ll work with modern technologies and tools that enable us to build scalable, reliable, and high-performing systems. Some of the technologies you&rsquo;ll be hands-on with include:


	Java (latest versions): Powering our core backend systems with a focus on performance and maintainability.
	Spring &amp; Spring Boot: Building modular, production-grade microservices with robust dependency management.
	Docker &amp; Kubernetes: Containerized deployments and orchestration for consistent, scalable environments across dev, staging, and production.
	PostgreSQL &amp; DynamoDB: A combination of relational and NoSQL databases to handle structured data and dynamic scaling needs.
	Elasticsearch: For fast, flexible full-text search and analytics across large datasets.
	AWS Cloud Infrastructure: Deploying and managing microservices using a cloud-native architecture that supports global scalability.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-14",

Senior Backend Engineer (Java) Job at Moniepoint




        
    

        
        Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors’ first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Backend Engineer (Java)

Job Location: Nigeria (Remote)

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Engineering at Moniepoint is an inspired, customer-focused community, dedicated to crafting solutions that redefine our industry. Our infrastructure runs on some of the cool tools that excite infrastructure engineers - kubernetes, docker etc. 
	We also make business decisions based on the large stream of data we receive daily, so we work daily with big data, perform data analytics and build models to make sense of the noise and give our customers the best experience. 


What You'll Be Doing


	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (product, design, QA) to define, design, and ship high-impact features.
	Design scalable architecture and implement performant backend services using Java and Spring Boot.
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code with a strong focus on reliability and testability.
	Integrate third-party services and APIs while ensuring robust error handling and graceful degradation.
	Continuously improve system performance and developer experience through automation and refactoring.
	Debug complex issues across the stack and proactively identify opportunities for technical improvement.
	Participate in code reviews, architecture discussions, and share best practices with the team.
	Document technical decisions, systems, and processes for future team reference and transparency.


Requirements
What We're Looking For:


	BS/MS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
	At least 5+ years of professional experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on backend systems.
	Expertise in Java and Spring Boot; solid understanding of modern backend architecture and best practices.
	Experience building and scaling web services, APIs, and distributed systems.
	Familiarity with Agile development methodologies and CI/CD pipelines.
	Proficient with relational databases and ORM frameworks (e.g., Hibernate).
	Exposure to frontend frameworks like AngularJS or React is a plus.
	Strong debugging skills and a methodical approach to problem-solving.
	Excellent communication skills and a team-first attitude.


Our Tech Stack
As part of our engineering team, you’ll work with modern technologies and tools that enable us to build scalable, reliable, and high-performing systems. Some of the technologies you’ll be hands-on with include:


	Java (latest versions): Powering our core backend systems with a focus on performance and maintainability.
	Spring & Spring Boot: Building modular, production-grade microservices with robust dependency management.
	Docker & Kubernetes: Containerized deployments and orchestration for consistent, scalable environments across dev, staging, and production.
	PostgreSQL & DynamoDB: A combination of relational and NoSQL databases to handle structured data and dynamic scaling needs.
	Elasticsearch: For fast, flexible full-text search and analytics across large datasets.
	AWS Cloud Infrastructure: Deploying and managing microservices using a cloud-native architecture that supports global scalability.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Secretary",
  "description": "Design Bricks Homes &amp; Property is a Nigerian real estate company focused on land sales, housing, property management, and real estate advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Secretary

Job Location: Egbeda, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a highly organized and proactive Secretary in Egbeda Lagos to provide administrative and clerical support to ensure the smooth operation of the office.
	The ideal candidate will manage correspondence, schedule appointments, maintain records, and support management with daily administrative tasks.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / BSc Business Administration, Secretariat studies or a related field
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong organizational and time management abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Ability to multitask and prioritize effectively.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Professional appearance and conduct.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Basic record-keeping and document management skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hrifeoluwa@designbricksproperty.com.ng using &quot;Secretary&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Secretary Job at Design Bricks Homes & Property




        
    

        
        Design Bricks Homes & Property is a Nigerian real estate company focused on land sales, housing, property management, and real estate advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Secretary

Job Location: Egbeda, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly organized and proactive Secretary in Egbeda Lagos to provide administrative and clerical support to ensure the smooth operation of the office.
	The ideal candidate will manage correspondence, schedule appointments, maintain records, and support management with daily administrative tasks.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / BSc Business Administration, Secretariat studies or a related field
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong organizational and time management abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Ability to multitask and prioritize effectively.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Professional appearance and conduct.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Basic record-keeping and document management skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Secretary" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
